subject: Mathematics
title: Spheres
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Spheres.

hook.text: Every point on a sphere is the same distance from the center. It's the 3D version of a circle.

buildup.text: Its volume is four-thirds pi r cubed. Its surface area is 4 pi r squared.

discovery.text: Archimedes proved the sphere formulas and considered it his greatest achievement.

twist.text: A sphere encloses the most volume for the least surface area—that's why bubbles are round.

climax.text: Planets, stars, and water drops all settle into spheres because gravity pulls equally in all directions.

punchline.text: The universe's favorite shape wastes nothing.

quiz.question: Why are bubbles and planets spherical?
options: Spheres minimize surface area for a given volume
